extraction.one_line_summary: LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel won stage 1 TTT with Astana by 2 seconds over Deceuninck-Quick Step, while ROGLIČ lost 40 seconds after Jumbo-Visma crashed on a wet corner.
tactical_patterns: VANGAARDEN Tejay pulled massive turns for EF, revealing team time trial depth despite lack of pure GC ambition.
Jumbo-Visma crashed on wet painted corner mid-course, losing 40 seconds; paint visibility in TTTs predicts crash risk.
VALVERDE Alejandro and QUINTANA Nairo sprinted final meters against each other, exposing internal Movistar leadership tension despite team result.
Astana held 1-second advantage at midpoint checkpoint over Groupama-FDJ, suggesting first 7km section rewarded pure power over technical handling.

rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: Took red jersey with TTT win, Astana fully committed to his GC leadership unlike multi-leader rivals.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Lost 40 seconds in crash plus visible left hip contusion, immediate physical damage beyond time loss.
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: neutral: Rode for LÓPEZ despite claiming sub-100% form post-Tour crash, questions about his true condition remain.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Movistar only 16 seconds back at 7th, keeps multiple GC options live despite internal tension.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Led Movistar through technical sections post-Tour, recovery faster than expected; statement ride about leadership claim.
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: positive: Immediately took front after crash to limit losses, organizational instinct under pressure remains elite.
rider_signals.KNOX James: positive: Led Ineos through technical corners smoothly, emerging as valuable domestique for stage racing support.
